title: Verbos reflexive
summary: Things you do to yourself: se lavar, se vestir.

teach.note: The reflexive pronoun — already seen in 'appellar se' (to be called, literally 'to call oneself').

grammar.title: The reflexive se
grammar.body:
**se** marks a verb as done to oneself: lavar (to wash something) vs. lavar se (to wash oneself).
Only 3rd person has this special form — for 1st/2nd person, the regular object pronouns already do the job: io me lava (I wash myself), tu te lava (you wash yourself).

teach.target: Le pueros se vesti pro le schola.
teach.roman: le pueros se vesti pro le schola
teach.english: The children get dressed for school.
teach.ipa: le pˈueros sˈe vˈesti pro le stʃˈola

canDo: Talk about washing up and getting dressed
Use the reflexive se with an -ar/-ir verb
